title: Why I Started Using goal measure With Every chronic pain group Reader

title: Pain Neuroscience Education in 30 Minutes - What I Actually Say
content: Most PNE frameworks assume you have an hour. I have 30 minutes between initial eval and the next participant. Here is the condensed version that actually sticks.

title: Preparing for the HTCC Exam - My Study Plan
content: The HTCC Human Trafficking and Professional Care exam is December 6. I am sitting it this cycle. Here is the resource list and weekly study schedule I built.
excerpt: A six-month HTCC exam preparation breakdown from an Occupational Therapy perspective.

title: Montana in October - Blue Mountain Hike Report
content: The larch needles were gold on Blue Mountain last weekend. Trail conditions, gear list, and why this hike is the best hour from downtown Missoula.

title: Draft: Occupational Justice and chronic pain group - What I Keep Coming Back To
content: chronic pain group disproportionately removes people from meaningful occupation. Draft thoughts on how I frame this with participants and what changes when I do.

title: Graded Activity vs. Graded Exposure - Picking the Right Tool
content: These two approaches get conflated constantly. Graded activity is quota-based; graded exposure is fear-based. Here is how I decide which one a chronic pain group participant actually needs.
excerpt: Distinguishing graded activity from graded exposure in pain rehab.

title: Five Books That Shaped How I Practice Occupational Therapy
content: From Lorimer Moseley's Explain Pain to Atul Gawande's Being Mortal - the reading list that quietly rewired my professional reasoning over the last decade.
excerpt: A reading list that shaped my Occupational Therapy practice.

title: Building MCWC Chronic Pain Program as a coach - Lessons From the Advisory Group
content: What I have learned sitting between the clinic floor and the engineering roadmap. The hardest part is not the features - it is keeping the participant's lived experience at the center of every spec.
excerpt: Reflections on advising a health-tech product as a working coach.

title: Cross-Country Skiing as Active Recovery - A Therapist's Take
content: Rattlesnake trails were perfect this week. Beyond the joy of it, classic-style XC skiing is a near-ideal low-impact aerobic and proprioceptive workout. Here is the Occupational Therapy lens on why.
excerpt: Why cross-country skiing makes great active recovery.

title: The Sleep Conversation I Have With Every Pain Reader
content: Pain disrupts sleep and poor sleep amplifies pain. I used to treat sleep as someone else's department. Here is the short, practical sleep hygiene conversation I now have at every initial eval.
excerpt: A practical sleep conversation for chronic pain group rehab.

title: What a Day on a chronic pain group workload Actually Looks Like

title: Cooking as Occupation - A Recipe-Free Reflection
content: Sunday cooking nights with Sasha are the most reliably restorative thing in my week. As an Occupational Therapy I keep noticing how cooking checks every box of meaningful occupation: planning, sequencing, sensory, social.
excerpt: Cooking through the lens of meaningful occupation.

title: Outcome Measures Are Only as Good as the Conversation Around Them
content: A perfectly administered goal measure means nothing if you do not revisit it. Here is the cadence I use to keep outcome data alive across an episode of care instead of letting it die at intake.
excerpt: Keeping outcome measures meaningful across an episode of care.

title: Houseplants, Routine, and Why I Recommend Them in Therapy
content: A monstera is a low-stakes recurring responsibility with a visible reward loop. For participants rebuilding daily structure after a pain flare, plant care can be a surprisingly good behavioral anchor.
excerpt: Using plant care as a behavioral anchor in rehab.

title: Draft: A Field Guide to Pacing for Readers Who Hate Pacing
content: Most pacing handouts feel like being told to slow down. Drafting a version that reframes pacing as protecting the activities you care about, not restricting them.

title: Trail Running Through a Missoula Spring - A Recovery Log
content: Coming back from a tweaked ankle, I logged eight weeks of return-to-run on the Kim Williams and Mount Sentinel trails. The Garmin data and what it taught me about graded loading on my own body.
